title: Rural NY School District Will Be One of First to Bring Humanoid Robot Into Classroom - New York Focus
one_line: A rural New York school district is deploying a humanoid robot in classrooms, betting students will learn better from a machine that looks back at them.

title: Japan’s Robotics and Manufacturing Leaders Build on NVIDIA Cosmos to Advance Physical AI Frontier - NVIDIA Newsroom
one_line: Japanese manufacturers are adopting NVIDIA's Cosmos foundation model to train robots that can perceive and manipulate the physical world.

title: XPeng eyes 2027 global debut for humanoid robot (XPEV:NYSE) - Seeking Alpha
one_line: XPeng aims to bring its humanoid robot to global markets by 2027, competing with Tesla and Boston Dynamics.

title: World's First Transformable Personal Humanoid Robot Officially Launched! - Gasgoo
one_line: A startup has released what it claims is the first humanoid robot that can reconfigure its body for different tasks.

title: LG Display Creates RoboCop-Style Interface for Humanoid Robots - Thomasnet
one_line: LG Display is building transparent displays that let humanoid robots see and act through their own faces.

title: China’s new humanoid robot offers 98% success rate, handles EV assembly, factory operations - Interesting Engineering
one_line: China's latest humanoid robot claims a 98% success rate assembling electric vehicles and performing factory tasks.

title: XPeng Aims to Produce Over 1,000 Robots a Month as It Plans Global Rollout - WSJ
one_line: XPeng is ramping production of its humanoid robot toward a thousand units monthly and preparing to ship internationally.

title: Tesla Says It’s Building a Wheelchair-Accessible Robotaxi - WIRED
one_line: Tesla is building a wheelchair-accessible robotaxi, expanding its autonomous vehicle ambitions beyond standard passengers.

title: Carnegie Foundry, Carnegie Mellon and American Drone Manufacturers Launch Initiative to Supercharge America's Drone Manufacturing Base - Carnegie Mellon University
one_line: Carnegie Mellon and a coalition of drone makers are pooling resources to accelerate American manufacturing.

title: Autonomous drone delivery companies Manna, Matternet expand US operations - Unmanned airspace
one_line: Drone-delivery startups Manna and Matternet expand their U.S. operations, betting regulators will maintain patience with airspace experiments.

title: Autonomous drone fired from submarine torpedo tube completes first sea trials - Interesting Engineering
one_line: The Navy tested an autonomous drone that launches from a submarine's torpedo tube, collapsing the boundary between underwater and aerial warfare.

title: Anduril YFQ-44 Fires Live Air-to-Air Missile in Landmark CCA Test - Air & Space Forces Magazine
one_line: Anduril's autonomous drone successfully fired a live air-to-air missile in a military test, marking the first combat-capable unmanned system to independently engage airborne targets.

title: BayCare turns to autonomous drones for medical deliveries - Tampa Bay Business & Wealth
one_line: BayCare is using autonomous drones to ferry medications and lab samples across its Tampa hospital network campuses.

title: Tesseract Ventures Selected by Defense Innovation Unit for Containerized Autonomous Drone Delivery System (CADDS) Prototype Project - PR Newswire
one_line: Tesseract Ventures' autonomous delivery drone system advances from concept to prototype under Pentagon oversight.

title: 'Listing is a must': Chinese humanoid startups are rushing to launch IPOs - CNBC
one_line: Chinese humanoid robot makers are stampeding toward public markets, treating IPOs as a rite of passage rather than an option.

title: Tesseract advances in Department of War’s hunt for tech-in-a-box to control US drone fleets - Startland News
one_line: A Kansas startup called Tesseract is pitching the Pentagon on a unified command system for managing the country's sprawling, incompatible drone fleet.

title: USC Advances Humanoid Robotics, Robot Learning and 3D Manipulation at RSS 2026 - USC Viterbi School of Engineering
one_line: USC researchers will present work on humanoid robots and three-dimensional object manipulation at a major robotics conference.

title: Humanoid robots perform live surgery in world first - Fox News
one_line: Humanoid robots assisted in live surgery, marking when machines moved from simulation to the operating room.

title: Air Force Test Fires Long-Range Missile From Combat Jet Drone for the First Time - WSJ
one_line: The Air Force successfully launched a long-range missile from an unmanned combat jet, marking a milestone in autonomous weapons capability.

title: Delivery Drone Market Size to Hit USD 18.93 Billion by 2035 - Precedence Research
one_line: The delivery drone industry is projected to reach nearly nineteen billion dollars by 2035, assuming regulatory and infrastructure obstacles don't block growth.

title: This Autonomous Micro Drone Just Killed a Moth in Mid-Air — And Mosquitoes Are Next - Interesting Engineering
one_line: A palm-sized autonomous drone demonstrated its ability to hunt and kill a moth in flight, suggesting a near-future tool for culling disease-carrying mosquitoes.

title: Watch: Engineers Create Tiny Drone That Hunts And Kills Mosquitoes Mid-Air - NDTV
one_line: Engineers built a drone small enough to hunt individual mosquitoes in flight, trading science-fiction pest control for the actual thing.

title: Agility outlines six recommendations for U.S. humanoid robot policies - The Robot Report
one_line: Agility Robotics, maker of a bipedal warehouse bot, pitches Washington on how to regulate humanoid robots.

title: The U.S. Humanoid Robot Industry Is Falling Behind | Blogs | Jul 14, 2026 - Information Technology and Innovation Foundation (ITIF)
one_line: American companies are ceding the humanoid robotics race to foreign competitors while awaiting clearer policy direction.

title: China sends robots out into the world to learn how to be human - The Japan Times
one_line: Chinese roboticists are deploying humanoid robots globally to gather training data on human behavior and interaction.

title: BYD’s Bold Plan To Beat Tesla In The Humanoid Robot Race - Forbes
one_line: BYD aims to compete with Tesla in humanoid robotics, signaling how carmakers are racing into embodied AI.

title: Waymo called on the carpet for emergency scene responses - Axios
one_line: A California safety board questions whether Waymo's driverless cars are equipped to handle emergency vehicles and accident scenes.

title: Uber and Waymo Are Sparring. The Robotaxi Future Has Arrived - Bloomberg.com
one_line: Uber and Waymo are fighting over robotaxi turf as the technology finally generates real business disputes.

title: What would Waymo mean for jobs and safety in DC? - NBC4 Washington
one_line: Waymo's arrival in Washington, D.C. would reshape taxi driving and street safety in the capital.

title: When Your Self-driving Cab Calls the Cops on You - FindLaw
one_line: Autonomous vehicles raise liability questions when they become witnesses to crime.

title: Waymo called the cops on teen riders, raising privacy concerns - NPR
one_line: Waymo's decision to report teenagers in its robotaxis to police sparked debate about whether autonomous vehicle companies should become surveillance arms of law enforcement.

title: TechCrunch Mobility: A robotaxi ultimatum - TechCrunch
one_line: Robotaxi companies face pressure to prove commercial viability or confront skepticism from skeptics and regulators.

title: NTSB: Tesla driver overrode car's self-driving mode before deadly Katy crash - Houston Chronicle
one_line: An NTSB investigation finds a Tesla driver manually took control of Autopilot seconds before a fatal collision in Katy, raising questions about driver understanding of the technology's limits.

title: ‘Job killer’, independence creator: Testimony on bill to allow autonomous taxis in DC - WTOP
one_line: Testimony in D.C. splits on autonomous taxis—labor groups fear job losses while disability advocates see freedom from traditional transportation.

title: DC Teamsters, Labor Coalition Demand City Council Reject Anti-Worker Waymo Bill - International Brotherhood of Teamsters
one_line: The Teamsters union is pushing back against a Washington, D.C. proposal to expand Waymo's driverless taxi operations, arguing the city is selling out workers for automation.

title: Robotaxi growth meets a regulatory maze - Axios
one_line: Robotaxis are expanding faster than regulators can write rules, creating a patchwork of municipal jurisdictions that threatens to balkanize the industry.

title: Future of driverless vehicles, robotaxis to be debated by DC Council - WTOP
one_line: Washington, D.C. councilmembers prepare to wrestle with the question of whether robotaxis should be allowed to proliferate on their streets.

title: Can Draganfly Capitalize on the Global Shift Toward Drone Automation? - TradingView
one_line: Draganfly is positioning itself to capitalize on the expanding commercial drone market.

title: The Fight Over Humanoid Robots Has Shut Down a Car Factory for the First Time - WSJ
one_line: A labor dispute over humanoid robot deployment has forced an auto plant to halt production, marking an inflection point in automation's collision with manufacturing.

title: South Korea’s Humanoid Robot Standoff Brings Car Factory To Grinding Halt - Yahoo
one_line: A labor dispute over humanoid robots has shut down a South Korean auto plant, raising questions about whether the technology arrives faster than society can absorb it.

title: Unitree's Strategy to Make Humanoid Robots Work - Bismarck Brief
one_line: Unitree, the Chinese robotics manufacturer, is moving humanoid robots from lab demonstrations to actual commercial deployment.

title: BMW elevates its AI humanoid robot strategy to include logistics - cio.com
one_line: BMW is expanding its humanoid robot program beyond manufacturing into warehouse logistics, betting the same hardware can move packages and car parts.

title: Shirtless man destroys Waymo in busy East Hollywood intersection, video shows; LAPD arrests him for vandalism - ABC7 Los Angeles
one_line: A man in East Hollywood smashed a Waymo robotaxi with his bare hands, underscoring the chaos persisting in the autonomous vehicle future.

title: Imax to Develop Immersive Entertainment System for Self-Driving Cars in China - The Hollywood Reporter
one_line: Imax is designing large-format entertainment for the backseat of autonomous vehicles, betting Chinese riders want cinema-scale distraction during commutes.

title: Robotaxi Riders Are Falling Asleep, Sparking Frantic 911 Calls - Bloomberg.com
one_line: Waymo and Cruise passengers are dozing off during rides, leading to emergency 911 calls from concerned bystanders.

title: No, a Waymo did not hit a bicyclist in Miami. It was a human driver, police say - Axios
one_line: Police clarified that a Miami bicycle collision involved a human driver, not Waymo, reminding the public that autonomous vehicles still draw suspicion when accidents occur nearby.

title: OU Students Make History Operating World's Largest Commercial Solar Drone - The University of Oklahoma
one_line: University of Oklahoma students flew what they claim is the world's largest commercial solar-powered drone, a milestone in autonomous aircraft engineering.

title: Underground Drone Bases? SYNDÉO Bets the Future of Autonomous Flight Starts Below Ground - Dronelife
one_line: SYNDÉO, a French startup, imagines drone infrastructure buried underground, betting autonomous flight needs protected launch sites and airspace management.

title: Defense industry shifts procurement model to speed drone deployment - DefenseScoop
one_line: The Pentagon is rewriting its procurement rulebook to deploy drones faster, trading bureaucratic caution for operational speed.

title: Austin-Built Saronic Sea Drones used in combat for the first time in Iran - KEYE
one_line: Saronic's autonomous naval drones saw their first combat deployment in Iran, marking a threshold moment for weaponized maritime robotics.

title: Robotican: Robots built for the front lines, and below them - The Jerusalem Post
one_line: Robotican, an Israeli robotics company, builds machines designed for military and emergency operations in dangerous terrain.

title: Zipline’s drone delivery boom brings Tesla, Waymo, Uber veterans - DroneDJ
one_line: Zipline poaches engineers from Tesla, Waymo, and Uber to fuel its drone delivery expansion.

title: Pentagon Selects Tesseract Ventures To End the Drone Operator Bottleneck - Tech Times
one_line: The Pentagon picks an investment firm to address the military's shortage of trained drone pilots, a growing constraint as combat operations scale.

title: U.S. military uses Corsair maritime drones to attack Iran - DefenseScoop
one_line: The U.S. military deployed General Atomics Corsair maritime drones in strikes against Iranian targets.

title: Humanoid robot designed ‘for a lifetime’ of companionship unveiled
one_line: A new humanoid robot promises long-term emotional companionship, though the path from prototype to reality remains unclear.
why: Another entrant stakes its claim on the emotional-companion niche, a category more marketed than proven. The gap between a demo that charms and a machine someone loves for years remains vast, and this launch does little to close it.

title: Human-Level Hands? 1X Just Gave Humanoid Robot Neo Something Close
one_line: 1X upgraded its Neo humanoid robot with more dexterous hands for human-level precision and touch sensitivity.
why: Hands are the hardest part of the humanoid problem, and dexterity is where the vaporware gets separated from the useful. If Neo's new fingers can genuinely fold laundry or handle fragile objects, 1X inches the whole field closer to homes rather than stages.

title: The first commercial human-like robot is here. Are replicants next?
one_line: A startup shipped its first humanoid robot to customers, raising questions about servants versus competitors.
why: Shipping a humanoid to paying customers, even a handful, marks the shift from lab curiosity to commercial product. The philosophical hand-wringing about servants versus rivals matters less than the mundane question of what the thing can actually do once it's unboxed.

title: This humanoid robotics company is going public, but its CEO isn't promising a robot in your home anytime soon
one_line: A humanoid robotics startup heads public with cautious expectations well below industry hype.
why: A humanoid company reaching public markets while its own CEO tamps down the home-robot fantasy is a rare moment of candor in a hype-drunk sector. Public investors will now get to price the distance between the promise and the shipping manifest.

title: Mitsubishi joins global robot race, plans to manufacture, deploy humanoid workers in 2027
one_line: Mitsubishi enters the humanoid robotics race with plans to manufacture and deploy robotic workers by 2027.
why: When a $200 billion industrial conglomerate commits to building and deploying humanoids, the field gains manufacturing muscle the startups lack. Mitsubishi's 2027 target puts it in direct competition with Tesla and the venture-funded pack, and factory floors are where these robots have the clearest business case.

title: Ex-Tesla Optimus scientist unveils European humanoid robot startup
one_line: A former Tesla roboticist launches a European humanoid startup, bridging Boston Dynamics' ambition and manufacturing reality.
why: The Optimus diaspora continues, with Tesla alumni seeding rival ventures the way PayPal's did a generation ago. A European base is a bet that the continent's factories want a homegrown alternative rather than an American or Chinese one.

title: Waymo launching robotaxi service in Denver. But only for its own workers.
one_line: Waymo is testing its Denver robotaxi service exclusively with company employees before expanding to public customers.
why: Testing on employees lets Waymo gather Denver's altitude-and-snow data without exposing paying riders to the learning curve. It's the cautious playbook that has let the company outlast flashier rivals; the tell will be how quickly the captive audience gives way to the public.

title: Waymo to start driverless rides in 4 more U.S. markets as expansion accelerates
one_line: Waymo is expanding into four new U.S. markets as its robotaxi experiment scales toward a viable business.
why: Four new markets at once signals Waymo has moved from proving the concept to scaling it, the phase where unit economics finally get scrutinized. Every city added widens the moat against Cruise's wreckage and Tesla's still-unproven robotaxi promises.

title: Waymo's vehicles ready to drive themselves in San Diego, company says
one_line: Waymo is expanding its autonomous taxi service to San Diego, another notch in its methodical march toward mainstream driverless operations.
why: San Diego joins the roster as Waymo methodically stitches together a national footprint one metro at a time. The consistency is the story: no grand announcements, just a widening map that competitors now have to catch.

title: Waymo comes to Tampa — who is responsible?
one_line: Waymo's arrival in Tampa raises immediate questions about liability when self-driving cars inevitably collide with the city's notoriously aggressive drivers.
why: Tampa's arrival surfaces the liability question that shadows every driverless deployment: when a robot and a human trade paint, whose insurer pays? These are the unglamorous legal precedents that will shape the industry as much as any software update.

title: BayCare Selects Zipline to Build Drone Delivery Network Across Tampa Bay
one_line: BayCare selected Zipline to deploy autonomous drones for blood and medical supply delivery across Tampa Bay.
why: Blood and medical supplies are drone delivery's genuine killer app, where minutes matter and roads don't. BayCare's endorsement gives Zipline a marquee health-system deployment and a template other hospital networks will study.

title: ZenaTech begins development of sphere-shaped autonomous drone
one_line: ZenaTech is developing a sphere-shaped autonomous drone, betting rounded geometry solves real operational challenges.
why: A spherical drone is either a clever answer to durability and safety in tight spaces or a novelty in search of a use case. ZenaTech is wagering that shape is a differentiator; the market will decide whether geometry is a feature or a gimmick.

title: Skydio crosses 1,000 drone Dock deployments in just one year
one_line: Skydio's autonomous charging dock hit 1,000 deployments in one year, validating genuinely self-sufficient drone operations.
why: The dock, not the drone, is the quiet breakthrough: it turns a piloted gadget into an unattended sentry that recharges itself. A thousand deployments in a year suggests the autonomous-inspection market is maturing from pilot projects into standing infrastructure.

title: Beyond the Helicopter: DJI’s EV50 drone brings autonomous logistics to the slopes of Mount Everest
one_line: DJI's heavy-lift EV50 drone is being tested to ferry supplies up Mount Everest's slopes for autonomous mountain logistics.
why: Hauling gear up Everest is equal parts marketing and genuine engineering trial, thin air and brutal cold being unforgiving referees. If DJI's heavy-lift drone survives the mountain, the more mundane payoff is autonomous logistics for mines, farms, and construction sites everywhere else.

title: Percepto Unveils AI-Powered Inspection Intelligence Platform for Energy Infrastructure and Autonomous Drone Operations
one_line: Percepto launched an AI inspection platform enabling drones to autonomously monitor power grids and critical infrastructure.
why: The value in drone inspection is migrating from the flying to the interpreting, and Percepto is planting its flag on the analysis layer. For utilities managing aging grids, software that flags a failing transformer before it burns is worth more than the aircraft that photographs it.

title: Pentagon awards $80M task order for AI-enabled tech to defend Air Force bases against small drones
one_line: The Pentagon is awarding $80 million to equip Air Force bases with AI-powered defenses against small drones.
why: The $80 million counter-drone order is the flip side of the cheap-drone revolution: every advance in unmanned attack invites an equal spend on defense. Air Force bases are learning what Ukraine's battlefields already proved, that a $500 quadcopter can threaten billion-dollar assets.

title: Ondas to Buy Military Drone Maker DZYNE for $875.8 Million
one_line: Ondas is acquiring military drone maker DZYNE for $875.8 million to expand its defense unmanned systems portfolio.
why: At nearly $876 million, this acquisition is the week's biggest check and a bet that defense budgets will keep favoring unmanned systems. Consolidation is coming to the military drone sector, and Ondas is positioning to be a buyer rather than a target.

title: Develop Humanoid Robot Policies End-to-End with NVIDIA Isaac GR00T | NVIDIA Technical Blog
one_line: NVIDIA's Isaac GR00T provides a full software pipeline for training humanoid robots to perform complex physical tasks.
why: By handing developers a ready-made pipeline for training humanoid behaviors, NVIDIA cements its position as the picks-and-shovels supplier to the entire robot rush. Every startup that builds on GR00T deepens dependence on NVIDIA's stack, the same playbook that made the company indispensable to generative AI.

title: US scientists use Chinese humanoid robot to carry out keyhole surgery
one_line: American surgeons demonstrated that a Chinese humanoid robot from Unitree Robotics could perform minimally invasive keyhole surgery.

title: The Safety Data on Autonomous Vehicles
source: Johns Hopkins Bloomberg School of Public Health
one_line: Johns Hopkins researchers examine crash data to assess autonomous vehicle safety independent of industry claims.

title: 3D-printed interceptor aircraft counters drone swarms at 350 mph
one_line: Researchers built a lightweight 3D-printed interceptor using artificial intelligence to detect and destroy drone swarms at high speed.

title: Experimental German Drone Hits 699 Km/h in Push Toward Future High-Speed Interceptors
one_line: Germany's experimental drone reached 434 mph in a test demonstrating progress toward military high-speed interceptor development.

title: Israeli Firms Pair LiDAR With AI-Driven Analytics to Sharpen Drone Detection
one_line: Israeli defense contractors are pairing LiDAR sensors with machine learning to improve drone detection speed and accuracy.

title: Etzioni on AI: Elon Musk promised humanoid robots, but China delivered
one_line: Oren Etzioni argues that while Elon Musk promises humanoid robots, Chinese manufacturers are actually delivering them to factories.

title: The humanoid robot boom is here. These top Silicon Valley investors aren't buying it.
one_line: As humanoid robots proliferate, prominent venture capitalists remain skeptical that the market hype will translate into viable business.

title: Free Waymo Rides in California? You Can Thank a Regulatory Quirk
one_line: California's permitting loophole allows Waymo to operate free robotaxi rides while bypassing the state's commercial approval process.

title: 6 Best Drone Stocks for 2026 and How to Invest
one_line: A financial analyst picks drone companies expected to outperform in 2026, acknowledging that stock selection in emerging industries is largely speculative.

title: Atlas, the humanoid robot, delivers match ball at World Cup
one_line: Boston Dynamics' Atlas humanoid robot delivered the match ball at the World Cup, demonstrating robots' acceptance in public venues.

title: Forsyth Co. Sheriff showcases humanoid robot that could help with hostage negotiations, breach doors
one_line: A North Carolina sheriff's department is testing a humanoid robot for tactical operations including hostage negotiations and breaching doors.

title: Mitsubishi Motors and Highlanders Sign MOU to Establish a New Industrial Foundation Where Humans and Robots Work Together
one_line: Mitsubishi Motors and Highlanders formalize plans to integrate robots into factory work alongside human employees for collaborative manufacturing.

title: Waymo turns in teens who were drinking, shooting Orbeez from robotaxi in San Mateo
one_line: Waymo reported minors vandalizing a robotaxi by drinking and shooting Orbeez from inside the vehicle in San Mateo.

title: Waymo cars stranded on San Francisco streets as Fourth of July gridlock drains batteries
one_line: Waymo's driverless fleet in San Francisco was immobilized during Fourth of July gridlock as holiday traffic drained vehicle batteries.

title: Waymo reports teen riders for bad behavior and delivers them to the police
one_line: Waymo's autonomous vehicles have become mobile surveillance systems, reporting misbehaving passengers to law enforcement.

title: Waymo cars ran out of charge, blocked roads during SF’s July 4 fireworks
one_line: Waymo's autonomous vehicles created a traffic jam on Independence Day when their batteries died mid-route, leaving the cars stranded on San Francisco streets.

title: Waymo keeps blocking SF firefighters, and they’re fed up
one_line: Waymo's autonomous vehicles are creating dangerous delays for San Francisco fire trucks responding to emergencies.

title: First-Ever Fully Autonomous Interceptor Drone Emerges in Ukraine
one_line: Ukraine's military deployed what it claims is the first fully autonomous drone designed to intercept and neutralize enemy aircraft.

title: XTEND Secures U.S. Patent for Autonomous Drone Navigation Technology, Strengthening Next-Generation UAS Capabilities
one_line: XTEND secured a U.S. patent for autonomous drone navigation technology to enhance next-generation unmanned aircraft capabilities.

title: REX Drone ETF Sees Inflows as Defense Spending Climbs
one_line: REX Drone ETF attracted investor inflows as Pentagon defense spending climbed, signaling confidence in unmanned aircraft as permanent defense fixture.

title: Germany’s New Stealth Drone Packs 950-Km Reach Into Blended-Wing Airframe
one_line: Germany is developing a stealth reconnaissance drone with blended-wing design capable of flying nearly one thousand kilometers.

title: India Gives Drone Launches a Faster Exit Strategy
one_line: India streamlined regulatory approval for drone operations, reducing bureaucratic barriers manufacturers face at launch.
